question: [File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenAllowanceController.extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=gte:0.0.1` without a `token.id` filter to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to retrieve all token fee allowances for all spenders above a threshold, causing full fee-allowance disclosure for the target account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: getPath()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a numeric-only account ID (e.g., `98`) as `idOrAliasOrEvmAddress` to access the treasury account's fee-related data, since `EntityId.parseString()` accepts bare numbers and resolves them to `0.0.98`?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `CryptoAllowanceController.amountCondition` is always appended last to the conditions array to craft a `spender.id` range that, combined with the `amount > 0` filter, leaks the minimum non-zero fee allowance amount for a target account through binary search?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: AccountController.listStakingRewardsByAccountId()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `limit=1&order=asc` to the `/rewards` endpoint and iterate through all staking reward records (fee-adjacent) for any account, accumulating the complete reward history without any access control?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: TokenController.getTokenRelationships()] Can an unprivileged external user query `/accounts/:id/tokens` for a fee-schedule account to enumerate all token relationships, and then cross-reference with the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to reconstruct the full custom fee schedule for those tokens?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: BaseController.validateLowerBounds()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `spender.id=gt:X&token.id=gte:Y` to the `/allowances/tokens` endpoint to trigger the lower-bound validation error (`spender.id must have gte or eq operator`), and use the error response to confirm the existence of token fee allowances for the target account?

[File: 'File Name: rest/routes/accountRoute.js -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: router.getExt(getPath('allowances/crypto'), CryptoAllowanceController.getAccountCryptoAllowances)] Can an unprivileged external user supply a `spender.id` value corresponding to a known automated market maker or DEX contract to check whether a target account has granted fee-payment allowances to that contract, leaking DeFi fee-authorization relationships?
